posts.title: Introduction
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

This is a tag-driven, Tailwind-based Ghost theme. Every content type — courses, lessons, videos, web series, projects, travel, products and more — is expressed as an ordinary Ghost post carrying an internal #hash tag that decides its layout and the collection it belongs to.

How these docs work

The docs themselves are demo content: each page is a post tagged #docs plus a section tag such as docs-getting-started. The left sidebar groups pages by section; the right rail is an auto-built “On this page” table of contents.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: What this theme is and how the docs are organized.

posts.title: Installation
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Install the theme

Upload the theme zip in Ghost Admin → Settings → Design → Change theme → Upload, then activate it. Locally, drop the folder in content/themes/ and restart Ghost so the templates map is rebuilt.

Import demo content

The demo dataset lives in the dummy-content/ folder, split into one importable file per module. Import the modules you want from Settings → Import/Export. Import course.json and lesson.json together so lessons resolve to a real course.

Set up navigation

Importing navigation.json writes the primary and secondary menus. You can also edit them by hand in Settings → Navigation.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Install the theme, import demo content, and boot Ghost locally.

posts.title: Routes & the templates map
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Ghost needs routes.yaml to know about the theme's collections and custom pages. Upload it in Settings → Labs → Routes.

Trailing slashes & dotted paths

Every custom route needs a trailing slash. Ghost rejects paths containing dots, so ads.txt and llms.txt are served via redirects.yaml instead of routes.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Upload routes.yaml so collections and custom pages resolve.

posts.title: The tag system
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Each post's first internal tag selects its single-post layout via the dispatcher in post.hbs. For example #video renders the video player, #course renders the course overview.

Internal vs public tags

Internal tags (name starts with #) are hidden from readers and used for routing and flavour. Public tags are visible and used for grouping — for instance a lesson's first public tag is its parent course.

Attribute tags

Families like #level-*, #duration-*, #country-*, #group-* and #event-* add metadata that templates and JS read at render time.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: How internal #hash tags drive layouts and collections.

posts.title: Containers & children
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Several types are containers: a course, a web series, a project and a trip are each a post that also carries a public tag. Their children point back by making that public tag their primary tag.

The container pairs

  • #course → #lesson children at /courses/{course}/{lesson}/
  • #webseries → #episode children at /webseries/{series}/{episode}/
  • #project → #project-detail steps at /projects/{project}/{step}/
  • #trip → #travel posts at /travel/{trip}/{post}/

When importing demo content, keep a container and its children together so nothing is orphaned.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}

posts.title: Collections reference
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Collections are defined in routes.yaml. Each maps a tag filter to a permalink and a template.

Index collections​

Top-level lists: /blog/ (#blog), /videos/ (#video), /courses/ (#course), /webseries/ (#webseries), /projects/ (#project), /travel/ (#trip), /products/ (#product), plus timeline, changelog, newsletters, shop, prompts, snippets and experiences.

Nested collections

Child collections (lessons-index, episodes-index, project-steps-index, travel-index) give children their nested permalink under the parent's public tag.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Every collection, its filter tag and its template.

posts.title: Pages & taxonomies
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Standalone pages (about, contact, resume, guestbook, sponsor, collab, bucketlist) render from page-*.hbs templates by slug.

Taxonomies

/tag/{slug}/ renders a single tag archive; /tags/ is the tile-pile directory of every public tag. Authors live at /author/{slug}/.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Standalone pages, the tags directory, and author archives.

posts.title: Theme modes
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

The theme ships eight modes — light, dark, salesforce, youtube, netflix, claude, twilio and neubrutal — resolved flash-free before first paint in default.hbs. The selection is stored in localStorage under swarnil-mode.

Admin accent & fonts​

An accent colour set in Ghost Admin → Design wins over every mode via inline custom properties on <html>. Body and heading fonts also follow the admin setting first.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Eight brand-flavoured modes and how to switch them.

posts.title: Custom settings
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

The theme exposes custom settings (see package.json) such as the default colour scheme, AdSense enable + publisher id, the GitHub repo, the OneSignal app id, and the web-series intro copy. Set them under Design → Site-wide.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Theme-level toggles exposed in Ghost Admin → Design.

posts.title: Build & validate
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Compile the CSS/JS with npm run build. Validate the theme against Ghost's rules with npx gscan ., and package a release zip with npm run zip.

Local Ghost

Local Ghost runs at localhost:2368. The templates map is cached, so restart Ghost whenever you add a new .hbs file.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: npm run build, gscan validation, and packaging.

posts.title: Performance & SEO
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

The theme preloads the font and stylesheet and defers scripts. To keep Lighthouse scores high: give images explicit width/height and responsive sizes, lazy-load below-the-fold media, and set long cache lifetimes at the CDN.

SEO​

Every collection and single post ships JSON-LD (components/json-ld) and an on-page table of contents. An /llms/ route serves an LLM-readable index.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Keep Lighthouse green: CSS, images, caching.
